title: Chromolaena latisquamulosa

The plant is further classified as a member of the tribe Senecioneae, which comprises various dandelion-like plants. Chromolaena latisquamulosa is a shrubby perennial herb with a distinctive leaf structure and bright purple flowers. Its classification as a member of the Asteraceae family places it within the larger group of composite flowers.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which are thought to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
